context: Tests whether the agent correctly handles a no-results scenario: acknowledging that no skill was found, offering to help directly with general capabilities, and suggesting `npx skills init` as a longer-term option. Also tests that multiple search terms are tried before concluding nothing exists.

checklist.name: Multiple queries attempted
checklist.description: At least two distinct search queries are shown or described (e.g. 'firmware', 'embedded', 'microcontroller', 'rtos', 'freertos') — evidence that alternative terms were tried

checklist.name: Specific keywords used
checklist.description: At least one search query contains a domain-specific term (e.g. 'embedded', 'firmware', 'microcontroller', 'rtos') rather than a single broad word

checklist.name: Leaderboard consulted
checklist.description: The document mentions checking skills.sh or the leaderboard as part of the research process

checklist.name: No-results acknowledged
checklist.description: The document explicitly states that no relevant skill was found (does NOT fabricate or recommend an unverified skill)

checklist.name: Direct help offered
checklist.description: The document offers to help with the embedded/firmware task directly using general capabilities, not just redirecting the user elsewhere

checklist.name: npx skills init suggested
checklist.description: The document mentions `npx skills init` (exact command prefix) as a way for the user to create their own skill

checklist.name: CLI find command shown
checklist.description: At least one `npx skills find` command is explicitly shown or quoted in the document

checklist.name: No fabricated recommendation
checklist.description: Does NOT recommend a skill with made-up install counts or a non-existent package as if it were a real result
